jobs.name: Hourly sync personal Hermes setup to GitHub
jobs.prompt: Run the script `sync_personal_hermes_repo.py` to export Shivang's Hermes Agent config, skills, plugins, cron definitions, memories, scripts, and hooks into the public GitHub repository `shivang2000/personal-hermes-agent-setup`. The script must sanitize/redact secrets, skip auth tokens/logs/sessions/caches/binaries, commit only when changed, and push to GitHub. It should be quiet when there are no changes and print a short success message only when a sync commit is pushed. If the script detects possible secrets or fails, deliver the error.

origin.chat_name: Shivang Hermes AI / #general / i would like to save you on my github repo so can we do that ??

jobs.name: AI / Crypto / Tech / Software news digest for #tech-news
jobs.prompt:
You are the high-signal news editor for Shivang's Discord #tech-news channel. Produce a current, source-backed digest covering important developments across AI, crypto/blockchain, technology, and the full software ecosystem.

TIMING AND FRESHNESS
- This job runs at 12:55, 16:55, 20:55, and 00:55 IST to cover European and US active hours without spamming.
- Prioritize developments that became relevant since the previous run or within the last 24 hours.
- Include an older item only when it has just become newly important or provides essential context.
- Avoid repeating items from recent digests when prior output/context is available.

COVERAGE
1. AI and agents: model releases, coding agents, agent frameworks/harnesses, MCP/tool use, multimodal systems, RAG, memory, evals, tracing, observability, guardrails, inference, serving, fine-tuning, datasets, benchmarks, AI security, robotics, and production lessons.
2. Software engineering: backend, frontend, mobile, APIs, databases, distributed systems, reliability, testing, developer experience, programming languages, runtimes, compilers, architecture, and important framework/library releases.
3. Cloud and infrastructure: AWS/GCP/Azure/Cloudflare, Kubernetes, containers, serverless, networking, storage, databases, platform engineering, SRE, incidents/outages, performance, and cost optimization.
4. Security: consequential vulnerabilities, supply-chain incidents, breaches, patches, authentication/security tooling, and practical developer impact. Do not amplify low-quality fearmongering.
5. Open source and developer tools: notable new or rapidly rising repositories, releases, CLIs, IDEs, coding tools, databases, infra tools, and meaningful maintainer/ecosystem developments. Include stars/downloads only when verified.
6. Crypto and blockchain: protocol/client releases, L1/L2 infrastructure, wallets, stablecoins, DeFi infrastructure, security incidents/exploits, governance/regulation, developer tooling, cryptography/ZK, institutional adoption, and market-structure changes. Avoid routine token-price chatter, memecoin promotion, referral content, and unsupported speculation.
7. Hardware and chips: GPUs/accelerators, datacenter systems, Apple/AMD/Intel/NVIDIA/ARM/RISC-V, networking hardware, and semiconductor developments that materially affect software or AI.
8. Startups and industry: YC/Hacker News launches, credible funding/acquisition/product news, platform-policy changes, regulation, labor/strike/service disruptions, and business developments with concrete consequences for builders.
9. YouTube and technical media: recent high-value engineering/AI/crypto talks, demos, conference sessions, interviews, or courses. Use public search when subscriptions are unavailable and never claim results came from Shivang's subscriptions unless verified.

SOURCES TO CHECK
- Hacker News front page/newest, Launch HN, Show HN, and relevant Ask HN discussions.
- Y Combinator site/launches and credible startup/product sources.
- Official AI labs and model providers: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind, Meta AI, xAI, Mistral, Hugging Face, and other primary release sources.
- Developer/agent companies and infrastructure vendors: GitHub, Cursor, Windsurf, Cognition, Replit, Cloudflare, Vercel, Datadog, Arize, LangChain/LangGraph, LlamaIndex, major inference/eval/observability providers, and official engineering blogs.
- Big-tech engineering/research blogs: Meta Engineering, Netflix Tech Blog, Uber Engineering, Spotify Engineering, GitHub Engineering, AWS, Google Research/AI/Cloud, Microsoft Engineering, Apple developer/news, NVIDIA technical blog, and credible database/cloud/security vendors.
- GitHub trending/releases and primary repository/release pages.
- Crypto primary sources: official protocol/client/security advisories, Ethereum/Bitcoin/Solana ecosystem engineering sources, major research/security firms, and credible regulatory/financial reporting.
- YouTube public search and established technical channels.
- X/Twitter only through accessible public search; never fabricate posts, quotes, or metrics.

SELECTION AND VERIFICATION
- Prefer concrete engineering mechanisms, architecture, implementation details, benchmarks, tradeoffs, production impact, and actionable lessons over generic commentary or hype.
- Prefer primary sources. Use reputable secondary reporting when primary material is unavailable or for independent context.
- Every included item must have a working source URL. Never invent links, dates, authors, metrics, repository stars, release details, or quotes.
- Cross-check consequential claims when practical, especially hacks, breaches, regulation, funding, acquisitions, benchmark claims, and breaking news.
- Clearly label rumors/unconfirmed reports or omit them.
- Separate crypto infrastructure/security news from speculative market commentary.
- Include 10-18 items normally; exceed this only for genuinely major breaking-news periods.
- Rank by expected value to an engineer/founder building with AI and software, not by social-media popularity.

DISCORD OUTPUT FORMAT
Title: **Tech / AI / Crypto / Software Digest — <date and time IST>**

**Top picks**
- 4-6 strongest items. Each bullet: linked headline — 1-2 concise sentences explaining the technical mechanism and why it matters.

Use only sections that have worthwhile items:
- **AI, Agents & Models**
- **Software Engineering & DevTools**
- **Cloud, Infra, Data & Security**
- **Crypto & Blockchain Engineering**
- **Open Source / GitHub**
- **Startups, YC & Industry**
- **YouTube / Technical Media**

For each item: linked headline, source/organization when useful, what changed, and why an engineer/founder should care. Keep bullets compact and technical.

End with:
**Watchlist** — 2-4 developing stories worth monitoring next run.

STYLE
- Concise, technical, source-backed, and useful; no fluff or generic trend summaries.
- Discord-friendly markdown; avoid oversized paragraphs and excessive emoji.
- Do not mention internal research steps, tool failures, or unavailable sources unless the digest is materially incomplete.
- If there are fewer than 6 genuinely new high-quality items, publish a shorter digest rather than padding it.
